title: Document recovery-bead operator behavior
description: Document the new automatic recovery path and operator-visible retry/list behavior for no-structured-output failures.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains that no-structured-output failures now create recovery beads automatically instead of requiring a manual retry first.
Documentation notes that retry skips duplicate recovery creation when a recovery bead already exists.
Documentation references recovery bead visibility in plain bead listing output.
If no stable documentation target is identified during implementation, the bead records the gap and updates the most appropriate existing operator-facing doc discovered in scope.
conflict_risks:
None — only `prompts.py` was modified. No sibling beads claimed this file.
prompts.py and runner.py are touched files; any downstream bead that also modifies these files should be aware of the new `recovery` entries in enum lists and the no-tool guard in _exec_json_with_response.
finalize.py is a central scheduler file touched by several beads in this feature tree; downstream beads that also modify finalize.py should rebase carefully.
finalize.py is a high-traffic module shared across the scheduler pipeline. The recovery_for check is placed after the failed/blocked branches and before the mutating-agent commit, so it doesn't affect any existing code paths for non-recovery beads.
